name: East Grimstead
overview: East Grimstead is a small village on the River Dun in Wiltshire, about 5 to 6 miles east of Salisbury, forming the civil parish of Grimstead together with neighbouring West Grimstead. It has around 70 households and historically no shops, public houses or schools, with Holy Trinity Church (a former chapelry of West Dean) and the East Grimstead Reading Room serving as community focal points. The site of a Roman villa lies a short distance to the south-east, and the disused Salisbury and Southampton Canal once passed south of the village.
character: A secluded, tranquil farming village of period cottages, old farmhouses and a village green, with strong community life despite very few facilities.
amenities: East Grimstead Reading Room and village hall (community venue and events)
Village green (annual village events such as the summer pig roast)
No village shop or pub - nearest everyday shopping in Alderbury and Salisbury

schools.name: Alderbury and West Grimstead Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 6 miles west, with access via the A36
From Dean station (about 2 miles east at West Dean) on the South Western Railway Wessex Main Line
From Dean station toward Southampton Central

prices.summary: A mix of detached houses, traditional cottages and old farmhouses; most homes in the SP5 area sell well into the mid-to-high hundreds of thousands of pounds, with larger period properties commanding more.

thingsToDo: Walking the lanes and bridleways around the Dun valley
Spotting the surviving Salisbury and Southampton Canal bridge near the church
Community events at the Reading Room and on the village green
placesToEat: No pub or cafe in the village; nearest pubs and dining in Alderbury, West Dean and Whiteparish
pros: Quiet, secluded rural setting close to Salisbury
Active village community centred on the Reading Room
Mainline rail access nearby at Dean station
cons: No shops, pub or school in the village itself
Car effectively essential for daily needs
Very small community with limited housing turnover
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a peaceful, close-knit Wiltshire village within easy commuting reach of Salisbury who do not mind relying on a car.
